content.content:
# Install and Set Up kubectl on Linux

## Overview
This is the official Kubernetes documentation for installing kubectl on Linux. kubectl is the command-line tool for interacting with Kubernetes clusters.

## Key Requirements
- **Version compatibility**: Your kubectl version must be within one minor version of your cluster (e.g., v1.35 client works with v1.34, v1.35, and v1.36 control planes)

## Installation Methods

### 1. **Install kubectl Binary with curl** (Manual)

**For x86-64:**
```bash
curl -LO "https://dl.k8s.io/release/$(curl -L -s https://dl.k8s.io/release/stable.txt)/bin/linux/amd64/kubectl"
```

**For ARM64:**
```bash
curl -LO "https://dl.k8s.io/release/$(curl -L -s https://dl.k8s.io/release/stable.txt)/bin/linux/arm64/kubectl"
```

**For a specific version (e.g., 1.35.0):**
```bash
curl -LO https://dl.k8s.io/release/v1.35.0/bin/linux/amd64/kubectl
```

**Install the binary:**
```bash
sudo install -o root -g root -m 0755 kubectl /usr/local/bin/kubectl
```

**Alternative (without root access):**
```bash
chmod +x kubectl
mkdir -p ~/.local/bin
mv ./kubectl ~/.local/bin/kubectl
# Add to $PATH
```

### 2. **Install Using Native Package Management**

**Debian/Ubuntu:**
```bash
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install -y apt-transport-https ca-certificates curl gnupg
curl -fsSL https://pkgs.k8s.io/core:/stable:/v1.35/deb/Release.key | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/kubernetes-apt-keyring.gpg
echo 'deb [signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/kubernetes-apt-keyring.gpg] https://pkgs.k8s.io/core:/stable:/v1.35/deb/ /' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install -y kubectl
```

**Red Hat/CentOS/Fedora:**
```bash
cat <<EOF | sudo tee /etc/yum.repos.d/kubernetes.repo
[kubernetes]
name=Kubernetes
baseurl=https://pkgs.k8s.io/core:/stable:/v1.35/rpm/
enabled=1
gpgcheck=1
gpgkey=https://pkgs.k8s.io/core:/stable:/v1.35/rpm/repodata/repomd.xml.key
EOF
sudo yum install -y kubectl
```

**SUSE/OpenSUSE:**
```bash
cat <<EOF | sudo tee /etc/zypp/repos.d/kubernetes.repo
[kubernetes]
name=Kubernetes
baseurl=https://pkgs.k8s.io/core:/stable:/v1.35/rpm/
enabled=1
gpgcheck=1
gpgkey=https://pkgs.k8s.io/core:/stable:/v1.35/rpm/repodata/repomd.xml.key
EOF
sudo zypper update
sudo zypper install -y kubectl
```

## Verification

```bash
# Check version
kubectl version --client

# Detailed version info
kubectl version --client --output=yaml
```

## Optional Configurations
- Enable shell autocompletion
- Configure kuberc (kubectl configuration)
- Install kubectl convert plugin

For complete details, refer to the [official Kubernetes documentation](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/tools/install-kubectl-linux/).

#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "=== Installing Kubernetes (kubectl + minikube) ==="

# Update package manager
echo "Updating package manager..."
apt-get update -qq

# Install required dependencies
echo "Installing dependencies..."
apt-get install -y -qq \
    curl \
    ca-certificates \
    gnupg \
    apt-transport-https \
    containerd \
    docker.io

# Add Kubernetes repository signing key and repo
echo "Adding Kubernetes repository..."
curl -fsSL https://pkgs.k8s.io/core:/stable:/v1.35/deb/Release.key | \
    gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/kubernetes-apt-keyring.gpg 2>/dev/null || true

echo 'deb [signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/kubernetes-apt-keyring.gpg] https://pkgs.k8s.io/core:/stable:/v1.35/deb/ /' | \
    tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list >/dev/null

# Update and install kubectl
echo "Installing kubectl..."
apt-get update -qq
apt-get install -y -qq kubectl

# Download and install minikube
echo "Installing minikube..."
curl -LO https://github.com/kubernetes/minikube/releases/latest/download/minikube-linux-amd64
install -o root -g root -m 0755 minikube-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/minikube
rm minikube-linux-amd64

echo "=== Installation Complete ==="
echo ""
echo "Installed versions:"
kubectl version --client --short
minikube version

input.name: linux-09bec348
input.content:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "=== Starting Kubernetes Cluster (minikube) ==="
echo ""

# Start minikube with docker driver
echo "Starting minikube cluster..."
export MINIKUBE_HOME=/root/.minikube
export CHANGE_MINIKUBE_NONE_USER=true

# Use docker driver for minikube
sudo -E minikube start --driver=docker --cpus=2 --memory=2048 --no-vtx-check 2>&1 | tail -30

echo ""
echo "=== Kubernetes Cluster Started ==="
echo ""

# Get cluster info
echo "Cluster Status:"
kubectl cluster-info

echo ""
echo "Nodes:"
kubectl get nodes

echo ""
echo "Pod Status:"
kubectl get pods -A

echo ""
echo "Minikube Status:"
minikube status

echo ""
echo "=== Kubernetes is Running ==="

#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "=== Starting Kubernetes Cluster (minikube - none driver) ==="
echo ""

# Start minikube with none driver (runs Kubernetes directly on host)
echo "Starting minikube cluster with 'none' driver..."
export MINIKUBE_HOME=/root/.minikube
export CHANGE_MINIKUBE_NONE_USER=true

# Use none driver for minikube (runs directly on host without virtualization)
sudo -E minikube start --driver=none 2>&1 | tail -50

echo ""
echo "=== Kubernetes Cluster Started ==="
echo ""

# Wait a moment for cluster to stabilize
sleep 3

# Get cluster info
echo "Cluster Status:"
kubectl cluster-info 2>/dev/null || echo "Cluster not ready yet..."

echo ""
echo "Nodes:"
kubectl get nodes 2>/dev/null || echo "Waiting for nodes..."

echo ""
echo "Pods:"
kubectl get pods -A 2>/dev/null || echo "Pods not visible yet..."

echo ""
echo "Minikube Status:"
minikube status 2>/dev/null || echo "Status not available yet..."

echo ""
echo "=== Kubernetes Installation Complete ==="

#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "=== Installing Kubernetes (kubectl + minikube) ==="

# Update package manager
echo "Updating package manager..."
sudo apt-get update -qq

# Install required dependencies
echo "Installing dependencies..."
sudo apt-get install -y -qq \
    curl \
    ca-certificates \
    gnupg \
    apt-transport-https \
    containerd \
    docker.io \
    conntrack \
    socat \
    git

# Add Kubernetes repository signing key and repo
echo "Adding Kubernetes repository..."
curl -fsSL https://pkgs.k8s.io/core:/stable:/v1.35/deb/Release.key | \
    sudo gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/kubernetes-apt-keyring.gpg 2>/dev/null || true

echo 'deb [signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/kubernetes-apt-keyring.gpg] https://pkgs.k8s.io/core:/stable:/v1.35/deb/ /' | \
    sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list >/dev/null

# Update and install kubectl
echo "Installing kubectl..."
sudo apt-get update -qq
sudo apt-get install -y -qq kubectl

# Download and install minikube
echo "Installing minikube..."
curl -LO https://github.com/kubernetes/minikube/releases/latest/download/minikube-linux-amd64
sudo install -o root -g root -m 0755 minikube-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/minikube
rm minikube-linux-amd64

echo "=== Installation Complete ==="
echo ""
echo "Installed versions:"
kubectl version --client
minikube version

#!/bin/bash

# Extract Kubernetes metadata

# Find kubectl binary
KUBECTL_BINARY=$(which kubectl)
KUBECTL_NAME=$(basename "$KUBECTL_BINARY")

# Get version
KUBECTL_VERSION=$(kubectl version --client 2>/dev/null | grep -oP 'v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -1)

# Find desktop entry on Linux
DESKTOP_ENTRY=""
if [ -f "/usr/share/applications/kubectl.desktop" ]; then
    DESKTOP_ENTRY="/usr/share/applications/kubectl.desktop"
fi

# Find icons - kubectl/kubernetes related
ICON_PATHS=()
if [ -d "/usr/share/icons" ]; then
    # Look for kubernetes or kubectl icons
    ICON_PATHS+=($(find /usr/share/icons -name "*kubernetes*" -o -name "*k8s*" 2>/dev/null | head -5))
fi

# Add common locations
if [ -f "/usr/share/pixmaps/kubernetes.png" ]; then
    ICON_PATHS+=("/usr/share/pixmaps/kubernetes.png")
fi
if [ -f "/usr/share/pixmaps/kubectl.png" ]; then
    ICON_PATHS+=("/usr/share/pixmaps/kubectl.png")
fi

# Since no icons exist, use empty array
ICON_PATHS=()

# Output JSON metadata
cat << EOF
{
  "binary_path": "$KUBECTL_BINARY",
  "binary_name": "$KUBECTL_NAME",
  "display_name": "Kubernetes",
  "desktop_entry": $( [ -z "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ] && echo "null" || echo "\"$DESKTOP_ENTRY\"" ),
  "icon_paths": $(echo "${ICON_PATHS[@]}" | jq -R 'split(" ") | map(select(length > 0))' 2>/dev/null || echo '[]'),
  "version": "$KUBECTL_VERSION"
}
EOF

#!/bin/bash

# Extract Kubernetes metadata

# Find kubectl binary
KUBECTL_BINARY=$(which kubectl)
KUBECTL_NAME=$(basename "$KUBECTL_BINARY")

# Get version
KUBECTL_VERSION=$(kubectl version --client 2>/dev/null | grep -oP 'v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -1)

# Find desktop entry on Linux
DESKTOP_ENTRY=""
if [ -f "/usr/share/applications/kubectl.desktop" ]; then
    DESKTOP_ENTRY="/usr/share/applications/kubectl.desktop"
fi

# Find icons - kubectl/kubernetes related
ICON_PATHS=()
if [ -d "/usr/share/icons" ]; then
    # Look for kubernetes or kubectl icons
    ICON_PATHS+=($(find /usr/share/icons -name "*kubernetes*" -o -name "*k8s*" 2>/dev/null | head -5))
fi

# Add common locations
if [ -f "/usr/share/pixmaps/kubernetes.png" ]; then
    ICON_PATHS+=("/usr/share/pixmaps/kubernetes.png")
fi
if [ -f "/usr/share/pixmaps/kubectl.png" ]; then
    ICON_PATHS+=("/usr/share/pixmaps/kubectl.png")
fi

# Since no icons exist, use empty array
ICON_PATHS=()

# Output JSON metadata
cat << EOF
{
  "binary_path": "$KUBECTL_BINARY",
  "binary_name": "$KUBECTL_NAME",
  "display_name": "Kubernetes",
  "desktop_entry": $( [ -z "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ] && echo "null" || echo "\"$DESKTOP_ENTRY\"" ),
  "icon_paths": $(echo "${ICON_PATHS[@]}" | jq -R 'split(" ") | map(select(length > 0))' 2>/dev/null || echo '[]'),
  "version": "$KUBECTL_VERSION"
}
EOF

#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "=== Installing Kubernetes (kubectl + minikube) ==="

# Update package manager
echo "Updating package manager..."
sudo apt-get update -qq

# Install required dependencies
echo "Installing dependencies..."
sudo apt-get install -y -qq \
    curl \
    ca-certificates \
    gnupg \
    apt-transport-https \
    containerd \
    docker.io \
    conntrack \
    socat \
    git

# Add Kubernetes repository signing key and repo
echo "Adding Kubernetes repository..."
curl -fsSL https://pkgs.k8s.io/core:/stable:/v1.35/deb/Release.key | \
    sudo gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/kubernetes-apt-keyring.gpg 2>/dev/null || true

echo 'deb [signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/kubernetes-apt-keyring.gpg] https://pkgs.k8s.io/core:/stable:/v1.35/deb/ /' | \
    sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list >/dev/null

# Update and install kubectl
echo "Installing kubectl..."
sudo apt-get update -qq
sudo apt-get install -y -qq kubectl

# Download and install minikube
echo "Installing minikube..."
curl -LO https://github.com/kubernetes/minikube/releases/latest/download/minikube-linux-amd64
sudo install -o root -g root -m 0755 minikube-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/minikube
rm minikube-linux-amd64

echo "=== Installation Complete ==="
echo ""
echo "Installed versions:"
kubectl version --client
minikube version

#!/bin/bash

# Extract Kubernetes metadata

# Find kubectl binary
KUBECTL_BINARY=$(which kubectl)
KUBECTL_NAME=$(basename "$KUBECTL_BINARY")

# Get version
KUBECTL_VERSION=$(kubectl version --client 2>/dev/null | grep -oP 'v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -1)

# Find desktop entry on Linux - check standard locations
DESKTOP_ENTRY="null"
if [ -f "/usr/share/applications/kubectl.desktop" ]; then
    DESKTOP_ENTRY="\"/usr/share/applications/kubectl.desktop\""
elif [ -f "/usr/local/share/applications/kubectl.desktop" ]; then
    DESKTOP_ENTRY="\"/usr/local/share/applications/kubectl.desktop\""
fi

# Extract display name from package metadata
DISPLAY_NAME="Kubernetes"
if command -v dpkg &> /dev/null; then
    # Try to get display name from dpkg package description
    PKG_NAME=$(dpkg -S "$(which kubectl)" 2>/dev/null | cut -d: -f1 | head -1)
    if [ -n "$PKG_NAME" ]; then
        DISPLAY_NAME=$(dpkg -s "$PKG_NAME" 2>/dev/null | grep -i "^description:" | sed 's/^description: //' | head -1)
        if [ -z "$DISPLAY_NAME" ]; then
            DISPLAY_NAME="Kubernetes"
        fi
    fi
fi

# Find icons - search in multiple locations
ICON_PATHS=()

# Search using dpkg -L to list package files for kubectl
if command -v dpkg &> /dev/null; then
    PKG_NAME=$(dpkg -S "$(which kubectl)" 2>/dev/null | cut -d: -f1 | head -1)
    if [ -n "$PKG_NAME" ]; then
        # Find .png, .svg, .ico files in package
        while IFS= read -r file; do
            if [[ "$file" == *.png ]] || [[ "$file" == *.svg ]] || [[ "$file" == *.ico ]]; then
                ICON_PATHS+=("$file")
            fi
        done < <(dpkg -L "$PKG_NAME" 2>/dev/null | grep -E '\.(png|svg|ico)$')
    fi
fi

# Search in standard icon directories
if [ -d "/usr/share/icons" ]; then
    while IFS= read -r icon; do
        if [ -f "$icon" ]; then
            ICON_PATHS+=("$icon")
        fi
    done < <(find /usr/share/icons -type f \( -name "*kubernetes*" -o -name "*k8s*" -o -name "*kubectl*" \) 2>/dev/null | head -10)
fi

# Search in pixmaps
for icon in /usr/share/pixmaps/kubernetes* /usr/share/pixmaps/k8s* /usr/share/pixmaps/kubectl*; do
    if [ -f "$icon" ]; then
        ICON_PATHS+=("$icon")
    fi
done

# Remove duplicates
ICON_PATHS=($(printf '%s\n' "${ICON_PATHS[@]}" | sort -u))

# If no icons found, use empty array
if [ ${#ICON_PATHS[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
    ICON_ARRAY="[]"
else
    # Create JSON array from icon paths
    ICON_ARRAY="["
    for i in "${!ICON_PATHS[@]}"; do
        if [ $i -gt 0 ]; then
            ICON_ARRAY="$ICON_ARRAY,"
        fi
        ICON_ARRAY="$ICON_ARRAY\"${ICON_PATHS[$i]}\""
    done
    ICON_ARRAY="$ICON_ARRAY]"
fi

# Output JSON metadata
cat << EOF
{
  "binary_path": "$KUBECTL_BINARY",
  "binary_name": "$KUBECTL_NAME",
  "display_name": "$DISPLAY_NAME",
  "desktop_entry": $DESKTOP_ENTRY,
  "icon_paths": $ICON_ARRAY,
  "version": "$KUBECTL_VERSION"
}
EOF

#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "=== Installing Kubernetes (kubectl + minikube) ==="

# Update package manager
echo "Updating package manager..."
sudo apt-get update -qq

# Install required dependencies
echo "Installing dependencies..."
sudo apt-get install -y -qq \
    curl \
    ca-certificates \
    gnupg \
    apt-transport-https \
    containerd \
    docker.io \
    conntrack \
    socat \
    git

# Add Kubernetes repository signing key and repo
echo "Adding Kubernetes repository..."
curl -fsSL https://pkgs.k8s.io/core:/stable:/v1.35/deb/Release.key | \
    sudo gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/kubernetes-apt-keyring.gpg 2>/dev/null || true

echo 'deb [signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/kubernetes-apt-keyring.gpg] https://pkgs.k8s.io/core:/stable:/v1.35/deb/ /' | \
    sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list >/dev/null

# Update and install kubectl
echo "Installing kubectl..."
sudo apt-get update -qq
sudo apt-get install -y -qq kubectl

# Download and install minikube
echo "Installing minikube..."
curl -LO https://github.com/kubernetes/minikube/releases/latest/download/minikube-linux-amd64
sudo install -o root -g root -m 0755 minikube-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/minikube
rm minikube-linux-amd64

echo "=== Installation Complete ==="
echo ""
echo "Installed versions:"
kubectl version --client
minikube version

#!/bin/bash

# Extract Kubernetes metadata

# Find kubectl binary
KUBECTL_BINARY=$(which kubectl)
KUBECTL_NAME=$(basename "$KUBECTL_BINARY")

# Get version
KUBECTL_VERSION=$(kubectl version --client 2>/dev/null | grep -oP 'v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -1)

# Find desktop entry on Linux - check standard locations
DESKTOP_ENTRY="null"
for desktop_dir in /usr/share/applications /usr/local/share/applications ~/.local/share/applications; do
    if [ -f "$desktop_dir/kubectl.desktop" ]; then
        DESKTOP_ENTRY="\"$desktop_dir/kubectl.desktop\""
        break
    fi
done

# Extract display name using multiple strategies:
# 1. Try to find from .desktop file Name= field
# 2. Try to extract from package metadata (dpkg/rpm)
# 3. Fall back to binary name capitalized

DISPLAY_NAME=""

# Strategy 1: Extract from .desktop file if available
if [ "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" != "null" ]; then
    # Remove quotes from DESKTOP_ENTRY for file reading
    DESKTOP_FILE="${DESKTOP_ENTRY%\"}"
    DESKTOP_FILE="${DESKTOP_FILE#\"}"
    if [ -f "$DESKTOP_FILE" ]; then
        DISPLAY_NAME=$(grep "^Name=" "$DESKTOP_FILE" | sed 's/^Name=//' | head -1)
    fi
fi

# Strategy 2: Extract from package metadata if available
if [ -z "$DISPLAY_NAME" ]; then
    if command -v dpkg &> /dev/null; then
        PKG_NAME=$(dpkg -S "$(which kubectl)" 2>/dev/null | cut -d: -f1 | head -1)
        if [ -n "$PKG_NAME" ]; then
            # Try to get from Summary or Description field
            PKG_DESC=$(dpkg -s "$PKG_NAME" 2>/dev/null | grep "^Summary:" | sed 's/^Summary: //')
            if [ -z "$PKG_DESC" ]; then
                PKG_DESC=$(dpkg -s "$PKG_NAME" 2>/dev/null | grep "^Description:" | sed 's/^Description: //')
            fi
            if [ -n "$PKG_DESC" ]; then
                DISPLAY_NAME="$PKG_DESC"
            fi
        fi
    elif command -v rpm &> /dev/null; then
        PKG_NAME=$(rpm -qf "$(which kubectl)" 2>/dev/null | head -1)
        if [ -n "$PKG_NAME" ]; then
            DISPLAY_NAME=$(rpm -q --queryformat="%{SUMMARY}" "$PKG_NAME" 2>/dev/null)
        fi
    fi
fi

# Strategy 3: Use xdg-mime or desktop database
if [ -z "$DISPLAY_NAME" ]; then
    if command -v update-desktop-database &> /dev/null; then
        # Desktop database tools might be available
        DISPLAY_NAME=$(grep "kubectl" /usr/share/applications/mimeapps.list 2>/dev/null | head -1)
    fi
fi

# Strategy 4: Fall back to capitalized binary name
if [ -z "$DISPLAY_NAME" ]; then
    DISPLAY_NAME="Kubernetes"
fi

# Find icons - search in multiple locations
ICON_PATHS=()

# Search using dpkg -L to list package files for kubectl
if command -v dpkg &> /dev/null; then
    PKG_NAME=$(dpkg -S "$(which kubectl)" 2>/dev/null | cut -d: -f1 | head -1)
    if [ -n "$PKG_NAME" ]; then
        # Find .png, .svg, .ico files in package
        while IFS= read -r file; do
            if [[ "$file" == *.png ]] || [[ "$file" == *.svg ]] || [[ "$file" == *.ico ]]; then
                ICON_PATHS+=("$file")
            fi
        done < <(dpkg -L "$PKG_NAME" 2>/dev/null | grep -E '\.(png|svg|ico)$')
    fi
elif command -v rpm &> /dev/null; then
    PKG_NAME=$(rpm -qf "$(which kubectl)" 2>/dev/null | head -1)
    if [ -n "$PKG_NAME" ]; then
        # Find .png, .svg, .ico files in package
        while IFS= read -r file; do
            if [[ "$file" == *.png ]] || [[ "$file" == *.svg ]] || [[ "$file" == *.ico ]]; then
                ICON_PATHS+=("$file")
            fi
        done < <(rpm -ql "$PKG_NAME" 2>/dev/null | grep -E '\.(png|svg|ico)$')
    fi
fi

# Search in standard icon directories using find
if [ -d "/usr/share/icons" ]; then
    while IFS= read -r icon; do
        if [ -f "$icon" ]; then
            ICON_PATHS+=("$icon")
        fi
    done < <(find /usr/share/icons -type f \( -name "*kubernetes*" -o -name "*k8s*" -o -name "*kubectl*" \) 2>/dev/null | head -10)
fi

# Search in pixmaps using find
if [ -d "/usr/share/pixmaps" ]; then
    while IFS= read -r icon; do
        if [ -f "$icon" ]; then
            ICON_PATHS+=("$icon")
        fi
    done < <(find /usr/share/pixmaps -type f \( -name "*kubernetes*" -o -name "*k8s*" -o -name "*kubectl*" \) 2>/dev/null)
fi

# Extract icon from .desktop file if available
if [ "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" != "null" ]; then
    DESKTOP_FILE="${DESKTOP_ENTRY%\"}"
    DESKTOP_FILE="${DESKTOP_FILE#\"}"
    if [ -f "$DESKTOP_FILE" ]; then
        DESKTOP_ICON=$(grep "^Icon=" "$DESKTOP_FILE" | sed 's/^Icon=//' | head -1)
        if [ -n "$DESKTOP_ICON" ]; then
            # Try to find the icon file
            ICON_FILE=$(find /usr/share/icons -name "${DESKTOP_ICON}*" 2>/dev/null | head -1)
            if [ -f "$ICON_FILE" ]; then
                ICON_PATHS+=("$ICON_FILE")
            fi
        fi
    fi
fi

# Remove duplicates
ICON_PATHS=($(printf '%s\n' "${ICON_PATHS[@]}" | sort -u))

# If no icons found, use empty array
if [ ${#ICON_PATHS[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
    ICON_ARRAY="[]"
else
    # Create JSON array from icon paths
    ICON_ARRAY="["
    for i in "${!ICON_PATHS[@]}"; do
        if [ $i -gt 0 ]; then
            ICON_ARRAY="$ICON_ARRAY,"
        fi
        ICON_ARRAY="$ICON_ARRAY\"${ICON_PATHS[$i]}\""
    done
    ICON_ARRAY="$ICON_ARRAY]"
fi

# Output JSON metadata
cat << EOF
{
  "binary_path": "$KUBECTL_BINARY",
  "binary_name": "$KUBECTL_NAME",
  "display_name": "$DISPLAY_NAME",
  "desktop_entry": $DESKTOP_ENTRY,
  "icon_paths": $ICON_ARRAY,
  "version": "$KUBECTL_VERSION"
}
EOF

kubectl api-resources 2>/dev/null | head -20 || echo "Note: No cluster connected, but kubectl is fully installed"
echo ""

echo "=== Kubernetes Successfully Installed ==="
echo ""
echo "Installation Details:"
echo "- Binary: $(which kubectl)"

result.extract_metadata_script:
#!/bin/bash

# Extract Kubernetes metadata

# Find kubectl binary
KUBECTL_BINARY=$(which kubectl)
KUBECTL_NAME=$(basename "$KUBECTL_BINARY")

# Get version
KUBECTL_VERSION=$(kubectl version --client 2>/dev/null | grep -oP 'v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -1)

# Find desktop entry on Linux - check standard locations
DESKTOP_ENTRY="null"
for desktop_dir in /usr/share/applications /usr/local/share/applications ~/.local/share/applications; do
    if [ -f "$desktop_dir/kubectl.desktop" ]; then
        DESKTOP_ENTRY="\"$desktop_dir/kubectl.desktop\""
        break
    fi
done

# Extract display name using multiple strategies:
# 1. Try to find from .desktop file Name= field
# 2. Try to extract from package metadata (dpkg/rpm)
# 3. Use xdg-mime or desktop database
# 4. Fall back to binary name

DISPLAY_NAME=""

# Strategy 1: Extract from .desktop file if available
if [ "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" != "null" ]; then
    # Remove quotes from DESKTOP_ENTRY for file reading
    DESKTOP_FILE="${DESKTOP_ENTRY%\"}"
    DESKTOP_FILE="${DESKTOP_FILE#\"}"
    if [ -f "$DESKTOP_FILE" ]; then
        DISPLAY_NAME=$(grep "^Name=" "$DESKTOP_FILE" | sed 's/^Name=//' | head -1)
    fi
fi

# Strategy 2: Extract from package metadata if available
if [ -z "$DISPLAY_NAME" ]; then
    if command -v dpkg &> /dev/null; then
        PKG_NAME=$(dpkg -S "$(which kubectl)" 2>/dev/null | cut -d: -f1 | head -1)
        if [ -n "$PKG_NAME" ]; then
            # Try to get from Summary or Description field
            PKG_DESC=$(dpkg -s "$PKG_NAME" 2>/dev/null | grep "^Summary:" | sed 's/^Summary: //')
            if [ -z "$PKG_DESC" ]; then
                PKG_DESC=$(dpkg -s "$PKG_NAME" 2>/dev/null | grep "^Description:" | sed 's/^Description: //')
            fi
            if [ -n "$PKG_DESC" ]; then
                DISPLAY_NAME="$PKG_DESC"
            fi
        fi
    elif command -v rpm &> /dev/null; then
        PKG_NAME=$(rpm -qf "$(which kubectl)" 2>/dev/null | head -1)
        if [ -n "$PKG_NAME" ]; then
            DISPLAY_NAME=$(rpm -q --queryformat="%{SUMMARY}" "$PKG_NAME" 2>/dev/null)
        fi
    fi
fi

# Strategy 3: Use xdg-mime or desktop database
if [ -z "$DISPLAY_NAME" ]; then
    if command -v update-desktop-database &> /dev/null; then
        # Desktop database tools might be available
        DISPLAY_NAME=$(grep "kubectl" /usr/share/applications/mimeapps.list 2>/dev/null | head -1)
    fi
fi

# Strategy 4: Fall back to capitalized binary name
if [ -z "$DISPLAY_NAME" ]; then
    DISPLAY_NAME="Kubernetes"
fi

# Find icons - search in multiple locations
ICON_PATHS=()

# Search using dpkg -L to list package files for kubectl
if command -v dpkg &> /dev/null; then
    PKG_NAME=$(dpkg -S "$(which kubectl)" 2>/dev/null | cut -d: -f1 | head -1)
    if [ -n "$PKG_NAME" ]; then
        # Find .png, .svg, .ico files in package
        while IFS= read -r file; do
            if [[ "$file" == *.png ]] || [[ "$file" == *.svg ]] || [[ "$file" == *.ico ]]; then
                ICON_PATHS+=("$file")
            fi
        done < <(dpkg -L "$PKG_NAME" 2>/dev/null | grep -E '\.(png|svg|ico)$')
    fi
elif command -v rpm &> /dev/null; then
    PKG_NAME=$(rpm -qf "$(which kubectl)" 2>/dev/null | head -1)
    if [ -n "$PKG_NAME" ]; then
        # Find .png, .svg, .ico files in package
        while IFS= read -r file; do
            if [[ "$file" == *.png ]] || [[ "$file" == *.svg ]] || [[ "$file" == *.ico ]]; then
                ICON_PATHS+=("$file")
            fi
        done < <(rpm -ql "$PKG_NAME" 2>/dev/null | grep -E '\.(png|svg|ico)$')
    fi
fi

# Search in standard icon directories using find
if [ -d "/usr/share/icons" ]; then
    while IFS= read -r icon; do
        if [ -f "$icon" ]; then
            ICON_PATHS+=("$icon")
        fi
    done < <(find /usr/share/icons -type f \( -name "*kubernetes*" -o -name "*k8s*" -o -name "*kubectl*" \) 2>/dev/null | head -10)
fi

# Search in pixmaps using find
if [ -d "/usr/share/pixmaps" ]; then
    while IFS= read -r icon; do
        if [ -f "$icon" ]; then
            ICON_PATHS+=("$icon")
        fi
    done < <(find /usr/share/pixmaps -type f \( -name "*kubernetes*" -o -name "*k8s*" -o -name "*kubectl*" \) 2>/dev/null)
fi

# Extract icon from .desktop file if available
if [ "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" != "null" ]; then
    DESKTOP_FILE="${DESKTOP_ENTRY%\"}"
    DESKTOP_FILE="${DESKTOP_FILE#\"}"
    if [ -f "$DESKTOP_FILE" ]; then
        DESKTOP_ICON=$(grep "^Icon=" "$DESKTOP_FILE" | sed 's/^Icon=//' | head -1)
        if [ -n "$DESKTOP_ICON" ]; then
            # Try to find the icon file
            ICON_FILE=$(find /usr/share/icons -name "${DESKTOP_ICON}*" 2>/dev/null | head -1)
            if [ -f "$ICON_FILE" ]; then
                ICON_PATHS+=("$ICON_FILE")
            fi
        fi
    fi
fi

# Remove duplicates
ICON_PATHS=($(printf '%s\n' "${ICON_PATHS[@]}" | sort -u))

# If no icons found, use empty array
if [ ${#ICON_PATHS[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
    ICON_ARRAY="[]"
else
    # Create JSON array from icon paths
    ICON_ARRAY="["
    for i in "${!ICON_PATHS[@]}"; do
        if [ $i -gt 0 ]; then
            ICON_ARRAY="$ICON_ARRAY,"
        fi
        ICON_ARRAY="$ICON_ARRAY\"${ICON_PATHS[$i]}\""
    done
    ICON_ARRAY="$ICON_ARRAY]"
fi

# Output JSON metadata
cat << EOF
{
  "binary_path": "$KUBECTL_BINARY",
  "binary_name": "$KUBECTL_NAME",
  "display_name": "$DISPLAY_NAME",
  "desktop_entry": $DESKTOP_ENTRY,
  "icon_paths": $ICON_ARRAY,
  "version": "$KUBECTL_VERSION"
}
EOF
